finding: Marked Decrease in Hepatic T2 Signal Intensity

differentials.name: Intravenous administration of ultrasmall superparamagnetic iron oxide
differentials.raw: Intravenous administration of ultrasmall superparamagnetic iron oxide

context: =  paramagnetic effect of intracellular iron deposition (ferritin, hemosiderin) ◊  Signal intensity of pancreas does not help distinguish between primary + secondary hemochromatosis! √  bone marrow of low SI (DDx: myelofibrosis) √  bone marrow of low signal intensity √  decreased T2 signal in spleen
